data.title: BGC names John Abularrage, JP Aubin, and Sean Windeatt as co-CEOs as Howard Lutnick steps down
data.description:
• BGC Group (NASDAQ:BGC) Wednesday named John Abularrage, JP Aubin, and Sean Windeatt as Co-Chief Executive Officers.
  • Howard W. Lutnick, who was confirmed by the United States Senate as the 41st Secretary of Commerce, has stepped down as Chief Executive Officer and Chairman of BGC (NASDAQ:BGC).

  • John Abularrage served as Co-Global Head of Brokerage and CEO of Americas for BGC Group (NASDAQ:BGC), where he was responsible for leading, developing, and growing BGC's brokerage business in the region.

  • JP Aubin has served as Co-Global Head of Brokerage and CEO of EMEA for BGC Group.
  • Sean Windeatt has served as BGC's Chief Operating Officer for the past 14 years and was appointed Co-Global Head of Brokerage in 2023.
  • Mr. Windeatt will retain his position as Chief Operating Officer, a role he has held since 2009.

data.title: UK's inflation rises to 3% in January
data.description:
UK's inflation rate increased to 3% in January from 2.5% in December, beating estimates of 2.8%.

The consumer price index decreased 0.10% in January over the previous month.

Core inflation rate decreased to -0.40% in January from 0.30% in December.

ETFs: (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB)

Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: Numinus Wellness announces CFO transition
data.description:
Numinus Wellness (NUMI:CA) (OTCQB:NUMIF) announced that Kelvin Yang will assume the role of interim CFO, replacing Melony Valleau, effective February 18, 2025.

Valleau will be concluding her interim tenure at the end of February.

Mr. Yang brings regulatory and compliance experience and will oversee the company's financial operations as it navigates its current strategic initiatives.

data.title: Japan's Infroneer and Accenture to establish JV to address critical infrastructure management issues
data.description:
Japan-based infrastructure service company Infroneer Holdings and Accenture (NYSE:ACN) to establish a joint venture aimed at enhancing productivity, increasing revenue, and accelerating the adoption of data-driven models through the use of advanced digital technologies.

The investment structure of the JV, Infroneer Strategy & Innovation, is 81%-owned by Infroneer and 19%-owned by Accenture (NYSE:ACN).

The JV will be established on April 1, 2025, and will also be responsible for cultivating specialized talent to manage IT investments and governance across Infroneer group, the companies said.

"This JV is Accenture’s first initiative in the construction industry in Japan, which wiInfroneerll not only accelerate Infroneer group’s competitiveness, but will also contribute to solving structural challenges that Japan is currently facing,” Atsushi Egawa, CEO of Accenture Japan said.

data.title: Anbio Biotechnology prices $8M IPO at $5 per share
data.description:
Global diagnostic company, Anbio Biotechnology (NNNN) priced its initial public offering of 1.6M shares at $5.00 per share.

The Class A ordinary shares have been approved for listing on the Nasdaq and are expected to commence trading on February 19, 2025 under the ticker symbol “NNNN.”

The company expects to receive aggregate gross proceeds of $8M.

The offering is expected to close on or about February 20, 2025.

Proceeds from the offering will be used for expansion of sales and distribution network in the strategically selected markets, research and development, and working capital and general corporate matters.

Anbio Biotechnology is dedicated to the advancement of medical technology and the provision of in vitro diagnostics products.

data.title: Japan reports larger-than-expected trade deficit for Jan, imports outpaces exports
data.description:
Japan’s trade deficit increased significantly to JPY 2,758.78 billion in January 2025 from JPY 1,766.54 billion in the same month a year earlier, exceeding market consensus for a gap of JPY 2,100 billion, as imports grew much faster than exports.

Exports from Japan rose by 7.2% y/y to JPY 7,863.75 billion in January 2025, picking up from a 2.8% growth in December while pointing to the fourth month of gain.

Imports to Japan increased by 16.7% y/y to a 26-month high of JPY 10,622.52 billion in January 2025, sharply picking up from a 1.7% growth in December and easily exceeding forecasts of 9.7%. It was the strongest rise in purchases since January 2023, with imports of mineral fuels rising 4.0%

Separate data showed, core machinery orders in Japan, a crucial gauge of capital spending, unexpectedly fell in December. Excluding those for ships and electric power companies, declined by 1.2% month-on-month to 889.3 billion yen in December 2024, the worst reading in four months. The latest reading also reversed from a 3.4% growth in November and defied market expectations for a slight 0.1% gain.

On an annual basis, private-sector machinery orders increased 4.3% in December, slowing from a 10.3% surge in November and missing estimates of 6.9%.

Additionally, Japan's manufacturers' sentiment improved for the second consecutive month, with the Reuters Tankan index rising to +3 in February 2025, up from +2 in January.

The Nikkei 225 Index fell 0.2% to below 39,200 on Wednesday, ending a two-day rally as investors reacted to disappointing economic data. The Japanese yen held its recent decline to around 152 per dollar on Thursday.

More on Japan

  • Japan’s core inflation rate surges to 16-month high of 3% in December

  • Bank of Japan raises policy rate to 0.5%, highest since 2008 amid sustained inflation and rising wages

  • Japan unexpectedly logs trade surplus, exports hits record high & imports also shows positive growth

  • BOJ's Dec summary raises expectations of rate hike soon; keeps policy rate steady at 0.25%

  • Japan’s Nov headline inflation rate rises to three-month high of 2.9%; core inflation tops forecast

data.title: Bank of Cyprus targets 4% loan growth and up to 70% payout in 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Bank of Cyprus (OTCPK:BKCYF) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Panicos Nicolaou highlighted significant achievements, including a record net profit exceeding €500M, an NPE ratio below 2%, and a 50% distribution payout for 2024 earnings. He emphasized strong shareholder value creation and raised the payout range to 50%-70% for future distributions.
  • The CEO revealed priorities for 2025, including navigating interest rate normalization, driving loan book growth, and strengthening franchise investments while maintaining strict risk and cost discipline. The bank expects high-teens ROTE on a 15% CET1 ratio in 2025.
  • CFO Eliza Livadiotou reported a 20% increase in new loans to €2.4B for 2024 and highlighted a cost-to-income ratio of 34%, despite inflationary pressures. She also noted strong liquidity and customer deposit growth of 6% year-over-year, reaching €20.5B.

Outlook

  • Management anticipates net interest income (NII) to decline below €700M in 2025, with stabilization expected in 2026 at over €650M, assuming rates remain at 2%. Loan growth is projected to continue at around 4% annually, supported by domestic demand and selective international expansion.
  • Non-NII revenue sources, including fees, commissions, and insurance income, are expected to grow at 4% annually. Insurance businesses are projected to achieve over 6% annual growth in income.
  • The bank plans to maintain a cost-to-income ratio around 40% in 2025, leveraging ongoing digital transformations to offset inflationary pressures.

Financial Results

  • Full-year 2024 profit after tax reached €508M, equivalent to earnings per share of €1.14, a 5% year-over-year increase. NII rose 4% year-over-year to €822M, despite a modest 3% quarterly decline due to lower ECB deposit rates.
  • The gross performing loan book expanded 4% year-over-year to €10.2B, driven by corporate and international lending.
  • The bank's CET1 capital ratio stood at 19.2%, with total capital at 24.0%, supported by 300 basis points of organic capital generation in 2024.
  • Operating expenses increased by 8% year-over-year, driven by inflationary pressures, IT investments, and professional fees. Cost of risk for 2024 was 30 basis points, reflecting a robust credit portfolio.

Q&A

  • Eleni Ismailou, Axia Ventures Group: Asked about deposit volume stability and capital returns. CEO Nicolaou indicated that deposit volumes and costs are expected to remain broadly flat in 2025, and the bank aims to reach the higher end of its 50%-70% payout policy soon.
  • Can Demir, Wood & Company: Queried about NPE sales, JCC income trends, and litigation provisions. CFO Livadiotou clarified that NPE sales had minimal P&L impact but were capital positive. She attributed JCC’s income trajectory to one-off costs and reaffirmed its value to the group.
  • Alfredo Alonso, Deutsche Bank: Requested clarification on capital targets and cost of risk. CEO Nicolaou reiterated high confidence in maintaining a high-teens ROTE with no evidence of asset quality deterioration.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns over NII declines, the evolving deposit mix, and litigation provisions, reflecting a slightly cautious tone. Repeated questions about excess capital indicated interest in potential shareholder returns.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing strategic achievements and future growth plans. However, responses to questions about hedging and JCC indicated measured caution.
  • Compared to the prior quarter, management’s tone remained consistent, with a focus on confidently addressing headwinds from rate normalization and market conditions.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted positively, with a raised payout policy of 50%-70% for 2025, compared to the prior quarter’s 50% target. Loan growth projections remained stable at 4% annually.
  • Analysts continued to focus on NII and capital returns, with increased interest in deposit dynamics and international loan book expansion.
  • Management’s confidence in achieving high-teens ROTE remained unchanged, but discussions around hedging and litigation provisions highlighted ongoing caution.

Risks and Concerns

  • Potential NII declines due to lower rates and deposit repricing were acknowledged, with management emphasizing hedging strategies and loan growth to mitigate impacts.
  • Litigation provisions and impairments on REMU properties were noted as challenges, though management remains optimistic about ongoing de-risking efforts.
  • Analysts expressed concerns about excess capital deployment and its impact on shareholder returns, alongside questions about JCC’s strategic fit.

Final Takeaway

Bank of Cyprus showcased robust performance in 2024, achieving record profits and strengthening its capital and asset quality metrics. For 2025, the bank is focused on sustaining loan growth, diversifying revenue streams, and maintaining cost efficiency, while navigating a challenging interest rate environment. With a raised payout range and strategic growth initiatives, the bank remains committed to delivering high shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Mitsui to buy 40% stake in Australia's Rhodes Ridge iron ore project
Wix.com reports mixed Q4 results; initiates Q1 and FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Wix.com press release (NASDAQ:WIX): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.93 beats by $0.32.
  • Revenue of $460.5M (+14.0% Y/Y) misses by $0.93M.
  • Creative Subscriptions revenue in the fourth quarter of 2024 was $329.7 million, up 11% y/y.
  • Creative Subscriptions ARR increased to $1.343 billion as of the end of the quarter, up 13% y/y.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities for the fourth quarter of 2024 was $133.7 million, while capital expenditures totaled $2.0 million, leading to free cash flow of $131.8 million.
  • Total bookings in the fourth quarter of 2024 were $464.6 million, up 18% y/y. Total bookings on a y/y constant currency basis were $466.2 million.
  • Creative Subscriptions bookings in the fourth quarter of 2024 were $325.2 million, up 15% y/y
  • Business Solutions bookings in the fourth quarter of 2024 were $139.4 million, up 25% y/y.
  • In addition, the Company provided its initial outlook for the first quarter and full year 2025.
  • On track to achieve Rule of 45 in 2025 at high end of outlook through continued innovation-powered growth and further FCF margin expansion.

  • As such, we provide outlook for the year and the first quarter on both as-reported and constant currency bases.

    As-reported As-reportedgrowth y/y FX impact Constant currencygrowth y/y
    Full year 2025
    Bookings $2,025 - 2,060 million 11 - 13% ~$45 million 13 - 15%
    Revenue $1,970 - 2,000 million vs. consensus of $2.02B 12 - 14% ~$34 million 14 - 16%
    Free cash flow $590 - 610 million 30 - 31% margin ~$25 million 31 - 32% margin
    Q1’25
    Revenue $469 - 473 million vs. consensus of $479.78M 12 - 13% ~$6 million 13 - 14%
